assignments.name: Add_to_Update_Included_Actuals_List
assignments.label: Add to Update Included Actuals List

assignments.name: Add_to_Update_Included_Forecast_List
assignments.label: Add to Update Included Forecast List

assignments.name: Assign_Award_Id_from_Existing_Update
assignments.label: Assign Award Id from Existing Update

choices.name: chcSkipActualsandForecast
choices.choiceText: Skip editing further Actuals and Forecasts

decisions.name: Check_Award_Record_Type_for_Baseline
decisions.label: Check Award Record Type for Baseline

decisions.name: Have_all_other_updates_been_completed
decisions.label: Have all other updates been completed?

Flow.description: Screen Flow that contains required elements for monthly reporting

formulas.name: fla_Monthly_Report_Summary
formulas.expression: IF({!rcdUpdate.Plan_for_Next_Month__c} = null, "Create Monthly Report Summary", "Edit Monthly Report Summary")

formulas.name: flaMonthPriorStartDate
formulas.expression: DATE( IF(MONTH(TODAY()) = 1, YEAR(TODAY()) - 1, YEAR(TODAY())), IF(MONTH(TODAY()) = 1, 12, MONTH(TODAY()) - 1), 1 )

Flow.label: GMS - Monthly Report Framework

fields.name: Complete_Test
fields.fieldText:
Monthly Update Completed


Thank you for submitting your monthly report. You can view your submission in the ‘View Monthly Updates’ tab.


Your report can be amended up until the 10th working day of the month. If any changes are required after this date, please request this via the Closed Updates table by selecting the report and clicking ‘Reopen report update’.

fields.name: IncompleteText
fields.fieldText: You haven't completed the full monthly update. Please click next to check what's incomplete and update.

fields.name: Copy_1_of_Initiate_Text
fields.fieldText:
Monthly Reporting Process

{!Get_Funding_Award.Name}

﻿

fields.name: Copy_1_of_ShfGuidancePicture
Initiate_Text
fields.fieldText:
Monthly Reporting Process

{!Get_Funding_Award.Name}

﻿

fields.name: ShfGuidancePicture
Update_Display
fields.fieldText:
You are editing the Monthly Report for {!rcdUpdate.Month__c}


Ref: {!rcdUpdate.Name}

Last Modified: {!flaLastModifiedDateSummary}﻿

fields.name: Lg_Text
fields.fieldText:
Baseline: {!rcdActualsListincUpdate[$EachItem].Parent_Baseline_Version__c}

Baseline End Date: {!rcdActualsListincUpdate[$EachItem].Parent_Baseline__r.Baseline_End_Date__c}

fields.name: Update_Exists_Error_DisplayText
fields.fieldText:
A monthly update already exists for this reporting month

Please return to the update list and edit the existing one. If it is past the reporting deadline you must request your PSO to reopen the update for editing.

subflows.name: GMS_Set_Funding_Award_Participant_PSO_as_Owner
subflows.label: GMS - Set Funding Award Participant PSO as Owner

textTemplates.name: txt_Actuals_And_Forecast_Instructions
textTemplates.text:
Step 2 – Reporting Actuals and Forecasts 

You will then be prompted to submit your actuals and forecasts: 

For Challenge Fund Grant Recipients 

You will enter: 

  • Actuals for each active phase 
  • Forecasts for each active phase, up to the required forecast horizon (e.g., three months ahead) 

For Strategic Partners 

You will enter: 

  • Actuals for the reporting month 
  • Forecasts, up to the required forecast horizon (e.g., three months ahead) 

 Data submitted will save on input and can be amended throughout the process if required. 


Important: 

All actuals and forecasts must be non‑cumulative. 


This means: 

  • Actuals = activity or spend in the reporting month only 
  • Forecasts = expected activity/spend for each individual future month, not running totals 

Examples: 

  • If you spent £10,000 in December and £6,000 in January, your January actual is £6,000, not £16,000. 
  • If you expect to spend £5,000 in March and £7,000 in April, your forecasts should be entered separately as £5,000 and £7,000, not a combined amount.

textTemplates.name: txt_Date_Clarification_Message
textTemplates.text: Please enter non-cumulative data only to represent progress made in this month only

textTemplates.name: txt_Fraud_And_Risk_Instructions
textTemplates.text:
Step 3 - Fraud and Risk Reporting 

After submitting actuals and forecasts, you will be prompted to report on: 

  • Fraud, Error, and Non-compliance instances 
  • Risk updates (new risks or changes to existing risks) 

You should complete these sections even if you have nothing to report. The portal will guide you through how to enter a “nil return” where relevant.

textTemplates.name: txt_HelpOnUpdateScreen
textTemplates.text:
﻿Monthly Reporting: What Grant Recipients agreed to undertake when they signed their Grant Funding Agreement  


Reporting Month: The reporting month always refers to the previous calendar month. Everyone reports against the same fixed period to keep data consistent. 


Baselines: Your baselines define what appears in your reporting screens, including active phases and required forecast periods. They only change through a formal update, so they control the structure of your monthly reporting.


Active Phases: Active phases are the phases that have been approved and are currently live in the reporting timeline. You must submit actuals and forecasts for every active phase each month, even if nothing happened.


Spend incurred: The cost your organisation has become legally obliged to pay, even if an invoice has not been received or money has not been paid to the provider of goods or services.


Noncumulative Data: Noncumulative reporting means you enter figures for each month separately, not running totals. This applies to actuals, forecasts, and A&A spend to ensure accuracy and prevent double‑counting.

textTemplates.name: txt_Helptext
textTemplates.text:
If you experience issues or need clarification: 


  • Contact your Delivery Support Manager (DSM) 
  • Use the support request function in the GRP 
  • Refer to any training materials or data dictionaries provided

textTemplates.name: txt_Instructions
textTemplates.text:
Overview of the Monthly Reporting Process 

Your monthly reporting journey includes the following stages: 


Step 1 – Monthly Update 

You will first complete a Monthly Update, which captures: 

  • A high-level summary of project progress for the reporting month. 
  • Narrative updates for the month just completed. 
  • (Challenge Fund GRs only) Grant A&A spend across all phases for the month. 
  • (Challenge Fund GRs only) Data on KPI 1 and KPI 2 to report progress on phases not yet submitted. 

Important: 

  • Values state activity and spend during the reporting month only. 
  • The A&A spend value only reflects spend incurred. 
  • The A&A spend value is the total value against all active baselines during the reporting month.  
  • All values entered in the Monthly Update are not cumulative. 

 

Step 2 – Reporting Actuals and Forecasts 

You will then be prompted to submit your actuals and forecasts: 

For Challenge Fund Grant Recipients 

You will enter: 

  • Actuals for each active phase 
  • Forecasts for each active phase, up to the required forecast horizon (e.g., three months ahead) 

For Strategic Partners 

You will enter: 

  • Actuals for the reporting month 
  • Forecasts, up to the required forecast horizon (e.g., three months ahead) 

 Data submitted will save on input and can be amended throughout the process if required. 


Important: 

All actuals and forecasts must be non‑cumulative. 


This means: 

  • Actuals = activity or spend in the reporting month only 
  • Forecasts = expected activity/spend for each individual future month, not running totals 

Examples: 

  • If you spent £10,000 in December and £6,000 in January, your January actual is £6,000, not £16,000. 
  • If you expect to spend £5,000 in March and £7,000 in April, your forecasts should be entered separately as £5,000 and £7,000, not a combined amount. 

 

Step 3 - Fraud and Risk Reporting 

After submitting actuals and forecasts, you will be prompted to report on: 

  • Fraud, Error, and Non-compliance instances 
  • Risk updates (new risks or changes to existing risks) 

You should complete these sections even if you have nothing to report. The portal will guide you through how to enter a “nil return” where relevant. 


Key Principles to Remember 

To ensure your monthly report is completed correctly: 


✔ Report for the correct month 

Monthly reporting always covers the previous calendar month. 

✔ Provide non‑cumulative data 

For all GR types: 

  • Actuals = this month only 
  • Forecasts = each future month individually 

For Challenge Fund GRs: 

  • Actuals/Forecasts are required per active phase 
  • Grant A&A spend is reported only in the Monthly Update, and is non‑cumulative 

✔ Baselines drive what you see 

Your reporting screens will reflect: 

  • Phase baselines (for CF GRs) 
  • Financial year baselines (for SP GRs) 

✔ Narratives should support your figures 

Use the Monthly Update to explain progress, issues, risks or challenges. 

 

If you require any previously submitted monthly reports to be edited or require a monthly report creating if a deadline was missed, you can do so by requesting this via the Closed Updates section of the GRP. 

 

If you experience issues or need clarification: 

  • Contact your Delivery Support Manager (DSM) 
  • Use the support request function in the GRP 
  • Refer to any training materials or data dictionaries provided

textTemplates.name: txt_Key_Principals_To_Remember
textTemplates.text:
Key Principles to Remember 

To ensure your monthly report is completed correctly: 


✔ Report for the correct month 

Monthly reporting always covers the previous calendar month. 

✔ Provide non‑cumulative data 

For all GR types: 

  • Actuals = this month only 
  • Forecasts = each future month individually 

For Challenge Fund GRs: 

  • Actuals/Forecasts are required per active phase 
  • Grant A&A spend is reported only in the Monthly Update, and is non‑cumulative 

✔ Baselines drive what you see 

Your reporting screens will reflect: 

  • Phase baselines (for CF GRs) 
  • Financial year baselines (for SP GRs) 

✔ Narratives should support your figures 

Use the Monthly Update to explain progress, issues, risks or challenges. 

 

If you require any previously submitted monthly reports to be edited or require a monthly report creating if a deadline was missed, you can do so by requesting this via the Closed Updates section of the GRP.

textTemplates.name: txt_Resubmission_Text
textTemplates.text:
Please adjust and resubmit your report for the following reasons:

{!rcdUpdate.Resubmission_Request_Details__c}

textTemplates.name: txt_Updates_Instructions
textTemplates.text:
Step 1 – Monthly Update 

You will first complete a Monthly Update, which captures: 

  • A high-level summary of project progress for the reporting month. 
  • Narrative updates for the month just completed. 
  • (Challenge Fund GRs only) Grant A&A spend across all phases for the month. 
  • (Challenge Fund GRs only) Data on KPI 1 and KPI 2 to report progress on phases not yet submitted. 

Important: 

  • Values state activity and spend during the reporting month only. 
  • The A&A spend value only reflects spend incurred. 
  • The A&A spend value is the total value against all active baselines during the reporting month.  
  • All values entered in the Monthly Update are not cumulative.
